data.description:
## Why
`test_code_review_ws5.py::test_security_rule_yaml_files_are_present_and_nonempty` only
checks one file is non-empty and another contains the substrings `rebar.builtin.security.`
and `rules:` (`:36-39`). Invalid YAML, duplicate IDs, malformed rule objects, unusable
patterns, or wrong severities all satisfy it. VALIDATED; a behavioral loader-based check is
largely already present in the sibling registration test and should be the anchor.

## What
Load the vendored rule files through the production loader and assert the exact detector
IDs, backends, languages, and severities, with at least one positive and one negative
fixture per rule family, and prove malformed vendored data fails closed.

## Scope
- `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws5.py`; may keep the presence check as an explicitly-labeled
  fast freshness guard.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Rules are loaded via the production loader; exact IDs/backends/languages/severities
      asserted.
- [ ] >=1 positive and >=1 negative fixture per rule family.
- [ ] Malformed/emptied vendored data fails closed during validation.
- [ ] `make test` green.
data.title: Validate vendored security-rule YAML through the production loader with fixtures
